_comment: Mutation testing on the trust-critical modules (issue #187, part of #178). Diff-coverage proves changed lines EXECUTE; mutation testing proves the tests would CATCH a bug. Scope is deliberately limited to the highest-stakes pure logic: trust derivations (app/trust), the manual-entry dedup matcher (app/server/listings/dedup.ts), and the write rate limiter (app/server/rate-limit). MEASURED BASELINE on this branch (off main): total mutation score 76.99% across 352 mutants (browse-glance 100, summary 97.18, rate-limit 84.62, dedup 71.88, incident-recency 54.74 — the last is dragged down by the Zod error-message string literals and TZ paths that the DB-free suite can't fairly kill). thresholds.break is set to 76 (rounded DOWN from 76.99) so it PASSES today on a green main and can be ratcheted up later as tests improve — it is intentionally NOT set above the measured score, which would false-fail a green main. To raise the floor, add tests that kill surviving mutants (see reports/mutation/mutation.html) and bump break.
